title: Twitter @HrantDavtian
summary:
One thing we noticed in previous versions is that the benchmark rewards the keyword search ability of visual retrievers.

Visual keyword search is strictly important (e.g. financial/legal docs), yet I'd love to see query-docimage pairs that require more intensive reasoning

title: Twitter @ManuelFaysse
summary:
Together, these findings enable us to train a model small enough to run query embeddings on CPUs with competitive performance - which we hope will unlock new use cases. Lots more insights in the paper:
https://t.co/mCKx3RooTN
(6/N)

title: Twitter @ManuelFaysse
summary: Since document image-query pairs are scarce, we show that the model can benefit from augmenting training data  with text only query-doc pairs. This is one more example of embedding capabilities transferring across modalities (e5-v, DSE, nemoretriever) ! (5/N)

title: Twitter @ManuelFaysse
summary: Beyond a resource release, this paper studies the factors that make or break good visual document retrievers (data regimes, resolution, alignement, attention). 🧪 (2/N)

title: Twitter @ManuelFaysse
summary: 🚨 We release ModernVBert - a small bidirectional ModernBert encoder trained to process image inputs alongside text. When finetuned for document retrieval, it matches the original ColPali performance on ViDoRe with almost 10x fewer parameters (250M). (1/N) https://t.co/QngitiBJZE

title: Twitter @ManuelFaysse
summary: @raphaelsrty @tonywu_71 Awesome work ! I'll write my questions in the Github issues! and already am spamming you with them haha

title: Twitter @nanwang_t
summary:
Impressive work by @OpenBMB  and the Tsinghua NLP group! https://t.co/ulXNNnSoMs
- The training dataset size has nearly tripled compared to ColPali. 360k vs 127k
- TextRAG performs best with longer documents, while VisionRAG excels with shorter ones! https://t.co/UulBPS5hrP

title: Twitter @CimolaiMarco
summary:
Introducing mcdse-2b-v1: a multilingual (🇮🇹 🇪🇸 🇬🇧 🇫🇷 🇩🇪) embedding model for flexible visual document retrieval.

Trained on MrLight/dse-qwen2-2b-mrl-v1 (Qwen2-VL) using the DSE approach. It's like ColPali but multilingual and single-vector.

- MRL: shrink embeddings from 1536 to https://t.co/W8GuYq08pe

title: Twitter @ManuelFaysse
Twitter @NielsRogge
summary:
Probably have the same conclusion regarding ColPali and the role of multimodal models like Gemini which can also "retrieve" relevant information from PDFs.

Based on my tests, ColPali was a bit hit-and-miss, seems to be better at slides than at arxiv PDF papers for instance

title: Twitter @jobergum
summary:
ColPali is a model checkpoint and, most importantly, a promising direction for effectively searching collections of complex document formats.  

Now, you can train a custom model, e.g., replacing the VLM with Qwen2-VL-7B-Instruct, which demonstrates even stronger document

title: Twitter @ManuelFaysse
summary:
Enough people asked - we obliged- here's the entire ColPali training set: https://t.co/Av8Lm0EsY4 ! 

We hope this can help bootstrap some ColPali finetuning efforts and we're eager to see cool work from the community !

title: Twitter @ManuelFaysse
summary: New ColPali finetune coming as soon as I get allocated on SLURM 🚀 It will be interesting to see if scaling that sort of data really helps for retrieval !

title: Twitter @jobergum
summary: ColPali (visual language model with late interaction scoring) is probably one of the most significant innovations for document retrieval, especially for some of the primary RAG use cases with real business value (e.g., chat with your PDFs).

title: Twitter @mervenoyann
summary:
yes 👏🏼 thank you 👏🏼 
I've been excited about this for a long while! nice to see I wasn't exaggerating 😄

title: Twitter @ManuelFaysse
summary: Awesome to see the amazing support speed ! It's now on us to roll out the next iteration of ColPali fine-tunes with better support for various document formats, types, and languages 🚀
